'''Keres II''' was a [[Water/Legends|frozen]] rock [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] of the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]'s<ref name="Byss" /> [[Koros sector/Legends|Koros sector]].<ref name="NOP" /> It [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Koros]]<ref name="ByssCorr" /> but had no [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Byss" /> The planet contained a number of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] deposits, which a [[Keres II mining outpost|mining outpost]] was set up to exploit. However, the [[Miner/Legends|miners]] working in the outpost suddenly abandoned the planet without explanation<ref name="TCM" /> by [[3643 BBY]].<ref name="Date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates/1|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "The Carbonite Mines" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the [[Star Wars video games|game]], the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref>

{{Gamemechanics}}
By that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], an individual affiliated with one of the two warring governments in the ongoing [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] had a companion of theirs travel to the planet. The companion explored deep into the [[Mine/Legends|mines]] and encountered a [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] that had been freed from an icy prison. The companion escaped the planet with a haul of carbonite and various [[Ore/Legends|ores]].<ref name="TCM" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Keres II was first mentioned in the second installment of ''[[Byss and the Deep Core]]'' a series of supplements written by [[Rodney Thompson]] for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' and released on the [[Wizards.com]] on [[April 14]], [[2005]].<ref name="Byss" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the Keres II, in grid square L-8.<ref name="Atlas" />
